developerPortal.quickStartDesc: Integrate FIVUCSAS authentication into your application in three simple steps.
developerPortal.step1Desc: Click "Register New App" above to get your client_id and client_secret. Configure your allowed redirect URIs and scopes.
developerPortal.step2Desc: Include the FIVUCSAS auth widget in your HTML page:
developerPortal.step3Desc: Exchange the authorization code for tokens on your callback page:
developerPortal.signInToManage: Sign in to manage your applications
developerPortal.signInToManageDesc: Create and manage OAuth 2.0 client credentials, view registered apps, and configure redirect URIs. Browse the Quick Start Guide below to learn how integration works.

widgetDemo.title: Auth Widget
widgetDemo.description: The FIVUCSAS embeddable authentication widget -- like Stripe Elements, but for biometric identity verification. Drop a single script tag into any website to add multi-factor biometric auth.
widgetDemo.liveDemoDesc: Try the widget in real time. The SDK communicates with the verify-app via postMessage and renders inside an isolated iframe.
widgetDemo.tryModalHint: Opens verification in a centered overlay
widgetDemo.tryInlineHint: Embeds verification directly in the page below
widgetDemo.architectureDesc: Three-layer architecture isolating sensitive biometric capture inside a sandboxed iframe. The host page never touches raw biometric data.
widgetDemo.layer2Subtitle: Manages auth flow steps inside the iframe
widgetDemo.layer3Subtitle: Biometric data stays within the sandboxed origin
widgetDemo.htmlDesc: The simplest integration. Drop the script tag and Web Component into any HTML page. No build tools required -- just a complete working page.
widgetDemo.jsDesc: Full programmatic control. Use the FivucsasAuth class directly for modal or inline embedding with promise-based completion.
widgetDemo.reactDesc: Complete React component with TypeScript types, error handling, and inline container support. This is the same pattern the FIVUCSAS dashboard uses internally.
widgetDemo.footer: FIVUCSAS dogfooding -- the platform consuming its own auth widget

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Body: By accessing and using the FIVUCSAS Identity Verification Platform ("the Service"), you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, you must not use the Service.
terms.s2Body: To use the Service, you must create an account with accurate and complete information. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must notify FIVUCSAS immediately of any unauthorized use.
terms.s3Body: By enrolling biometric data (face, voice, fingerprint, or NFC document), you explicitly consent to the collection, processing, and storage of your biometric information for identity verification purposes. Biometric data is classified as sensitive personal data under KVKK (Law No. 6698) and is processed only with your informed, explicit consent. You may withdraw consent at any time by deleting your enrollment data.
terms.s4Body: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. You shall not: (a) attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble any part of the Service; (b) use the Service to develop competing products; (c) attempt to gain unauthorized access to other accounts or systems; (d) upload malicious code or interfere with the Service's operation; (e) impersonate another person during biometric enrollment.
terms.s5Body: All intellectual property rights in the Service, including but not limited to software, algorithms, biometric processing models, documentation, and trademarks, are owned by FIVUCSAS and Marmara University. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Service in accordance with these Terms.
terms.s6Body: FIVUCSAS strives to maintain 99.9% service availability. However, the Service is provided "as is" and "as available." We reserve the right to perform scheduled maintenance, updates, and modifications. We do not guarantee uninterrupted access and shall not be liable for any downtime or service disruptions.
terms.s7Body: To the maximum extent permitted by Turkish law, FIVUCSAS, its affiliates, and Marmara University shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the Service, including but not limited to loss of data, unauthorized access, or service interruptions. Our total liability shall not exceed the amount you have paid for the Service in the twelve months preceding the claim.
terms.s8Body: Either party may terminate this agreement at any time. FIVUCSAS may suspend or terminate your access if you violate these Terms or engage in fraudulent or abusive behavior. Upon termination, all biometric enrollment data will be permanently deleted within 30 days. Your obligations regarding intellectual property and limitation of liability survive termination.
terms.s9Body: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Turkey. Any disputes arising from or relating to these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Istanbul, Turkey.
terms.s10Body: For questions about these Terms of Service, contact: bilgisayar@marmara.edu.tr

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Body: We collect the following categories of data to provide identity verification services:
privacy.s1Biometric: Biometric Data: Face embeddings (512-dimensional vectors), voice prints (256-dimensional vectors), fingerprint templates (WebAuthn public key credentials), and NFC document serial numbers.
privacy.s1Personal: Personal Data: Full name, email address, phone number (optional), date of birth (from ID documents), and profile photograph.
privacy.s1Technical: Technical Data: IP address, device information, browser type, login timestamps, and session metadata for security and audit purposes.
privacy.s2Title: 2. How We Process and Store Data
privacy.s2Body: All biometric data is processed using state-of-the-art machine learning models and stored as mathematical vector embeddings that cannot be reverse-engineered into original biometric samples. Data at rest is encrypted using AES-256 encryption. All data in transit is protected with TLS 1.3. Face and voice embeddings are stored in PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. WebAuthn credentials use ECDSA P-256 public key cryptography. Biometric templates are never stored as raw images or audio recordings.
privacy.s3Body: Biometric enrollment data is retained for the duration of your active account. Audit logs are retained for a minimum of 2 years as required by Turkish regulations. Upon account deletion or consent withdrawal, all biometric templates and personal data are permanently and irreversibly erased within 30 days. You may delete individual enrollment methods at any time through the Settings page.
privacy.s4Body: All personal and biometric data processing is conducted in compliance with the Turkish Personal Data Protection Law (Kisisel Verilerin Korunmasi Kanunu, Law No. 6698). Biometric data is classified as "special category personal data" under Article 6 of KVKK and is processed only with your explicit consent. We maintain a data processing inventory (VERBiS registration) and implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures as required by the law. You may file complaints with the Personal Data Protection Authority (KVKK Kurumu) at kvkk.gov.tr.
privacy.s5Body: For users located in the European Union or European Economic Area, we additionally comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679. We process biometric data under Article 9(2)(a) (explicit consent). Where GDPR applies, it is interpreted alongside KVKK, and the higher standard of protection prevails.
privacy.s6Body: Under KVKK (Article 11) and GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.s6Right1: Right of Access: Request information about what personal data we hold and how it is processed.
privacy.s6Right2: Right to Rectification: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
privacy.s6Right3: Right to Erasure: Request deletion of your personal and biometric data at any time.
privacy.s6Right4: Right to Data Portability: Receive your personal data in a structured, machine-readable format.
privacy.s6Right5: Right to Withdraw Consent: Withdraw your consent for biometric data processing at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.
privacy.s6Right6: Right to Object: Object to automated decision-making based solely on biometric data.
privacy.s7Body: Biometric data is never shared with, sold to, or disclosed to third parties. Personal data may be shared with tenant organizations solely as required to provide identity verification services, and only to the extent authorized by your consent. We do not use biometric data for advertising, profiling, or any purpose beyond identity verification.
privacy.s8Body: We implement comprehensive security measures including: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit, row-level security in PostgreSQL for multi-tenant isolation, rate limiting and fail2ban for brute-force protection, JWT with refresh token rotation, regular security audits, and SOC 2-aligned operational practices.
privacy.s9Body: For privacy-related inquiries, to exercise your data rights, or to contact our Data Protection Officer, please email: bilgisayar@marmara.edu.tr. We will respond to all data subject requests within 30 days as required by KVKK.
